title: AI ROI Calculator for Operations Teams
subtitle: Stop guessing whether automation is worth it. Build a business case with real numbers and a real payback period.

inputs.label: Volume per Month
inputs.hint: How many times this process runs monthly (orders, invoices, onboarding tasks).

inputs.label: Time per Unit
inputs.hint: Total human time across all handoffs, not just one person's touch.

inputs.label: Fully Loaded Hourly Cost
inputs.hint: Salary plus benefits plus overhead. If unsure, use 1.3x base hourly rate.

inputs.label: Current Error Rate
inputs.hint: Percent of units that need rework. Most teams undercount this.

inputs.label: Cost per Error
inputs.hint: Rework time, customer impact, refunds, and goodwill.

inputs.label: Automation Build Cost
inputs.hint: One-time cost to design, build, and deploy the automation.

inputs.label: Monthly Maintenance Cost
inputs.hint: Ongoing support, hosting, model usage, and tweaks.

recommendations.text: Green light. Payback under 6 months puts this in the top tier of automation projects. Scope it, build it, and move to the next workflow on your list.

recommendations.when: build_cost / Math.max(1, (((volume_per_month * (time_per_unit / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.85)) - monthly_maintenance)) > 6 && build_cost / Math.max(1, (((volume_per_month * (time_per_unit / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.85)) - monthly_maintenance)) <= 12
recommendations.text: Solid business case. Payback inside 12 months is worth building. Tighten the scope so the first version ships in 30 days and you start compounding savings.

recommendations.when: build_cost / Math.max(1, (((volume_per_month * (time_per_unit / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.85)) - monthly_maintenance)) > 12 && build_cost / Math.max(1, (((volume_per_month * (time_per_unit / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.85)) - monthly_maintenance)) <= 18
recommendations.text: Marginal. Payback between 12 and 18 months means you need a cheaper build or a higher-volume process. Look for a similar workflow that runs 2x more often.
Wrong process to start with. Payback over 18 months rarely survives the first year. Pick a workflow with higher volume or a more expensive error profile.
Error cost is beating labor cost. Automation ROI here is really about quality control. Prioritize validation, schema checks, and human-in-the-loop review gates over raw speed.
You are freeing up more than one full-time equivalent of capacity. Decide now whether those hours get reinvested into growth work or taken out as cost savings. Drifting on that call kills the ROI.

capture_cta.headline: Want help building the automation, not just the business case?
capture_cta.description: Send me your top 3 workflows and the numbers you just ran. I will tell you which one to build first, what it should cost, and how fast it can ship.
